key_themes: Strong 2025 earnings beats with 43% adj. EPS growth and record underwriting income
Successful repositioning, ROE target beat, Fitch upgrades, and robust capital returns
CEO succession announcement triggered selloff and YTD price weakness
Hold consensus from analysts with moderate ~10-11% upside to ~$87 targets
sources_checked: AIG Investor Relations / Earnings Releases / Annual Report
summary: AIG delivered excellent FY 2025 results with adjusted after-tax income per share up 43% to $7.09, underwriting income rising 22% to over $2.3 billion (first time above $2B since 2008 excluding divestitures), improved combined ratios, 11.1% ROE exceeding targets, and $6.8 billion returned to shareholders.[[1]](https://www.aig.com/home/investor-relations/aig-2025-annual-report)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/american-international-group-inc-aig-210308507.html)[[3]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/AIG/earnings/) This reflects a successful multi-year turnaround with positive 2026 guidance on premium growth. Offsetting factors include a ~7% stock drop in January on CEO Peter Zaffino’s planned transition (to Executive Chair with new CEO from Aon), recent price action off December 2025 highs near $87 down to ~$78-79, a Q4 revenue miss, and predominantly Hold/Neutral analyst ratings.[[4]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AIG/)[[5]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/AIG/chart/)[[6]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/why-aig-aig-shares-getting-200616366.html) The biggest risk is leadership transition uncertainty plus exposure to insurance market cycles, catastrophes, interest rates, and economic conditions.

recent_posts.highlight: $AIG flagged in algorithmic scan for crossing bullish trend EMAs and monthly S/R levels on multiple timeframes.
Shares dropped sharply (~7%) on announcement of long-time CEO Peter Zaffino transitioning; investors appeared unhappy with leadership change timing.
Mizuho maintains Neutral, slightly lowers target to $84; overall consensus Hold with average target ~$87 from current ~$79 levels.
